overview.text: NVIDIA is a Santa Clara, California semiconductor and computing company founded on April 5, 1993 by Jensen Huang, Chris Malachowsky and Curtis Priem. Originally a graphics-chip maker for video games, it has become the dominant supplier of GPUs used to train and run AI models and now also publishes its own open AI models under the Nemotron line.

what_theyve_done.text: NVIDIA was founded in 1993 with $40,000 in starting capital and nearly failed after its first product, the NV1, missed the market before the company rebuilt around the RIVA 128, which sold about a million units within four months in 1997.

citations.quote: sold about a million RIVA 128 units within four months

what_theyve_done.text: By 2025 it controlled more than 80% of the market for GPUs used in training and deploying AI models, and it has since extended into publishing its own AI model line, Nemotron.

citations.quote: controlled more than 80% of the market for GPUs

what_theyve_done.text: On June 4, 2026 NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Ultra, a 550-billion-parameter (55-billion-active) Mixture-of-Experts hybrid Mamba-Attention model with a 1-million-token context window, released open-weight with training checkpoints and datasets included.

strengths.text: NVIDIA reports Nemotron 3 Ultra matching state-of-the-art open models on accuracy while delivering up to 5.9x higher inference throughput on some benchmarks, plus strong long-context retrieval at its 1-million-token window.
It releases the full training stack behind Nemotron models -- pretrained, post-trained and quantized checkpoints plus the underlying datasets -- rather than weights alone.

citations.quote: releasing the pre-trained, post-trained, and quantized checkpoints

current_focus.text: NVIDIA's current AI-model focus is Nemotron 3 Ultra, built for long-running agentic workloads with inference-time reasoning budget control, released open-weight alongside its full training data and post-training methodology combining supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning and multi-teacher distillation.

citations.quote: Supports inference time reasoning budget control
